clue: The most expensive spice in the world by weight, harvested by hand from crocus flowers, is ___.
Its smell lingers for days, so hotels and public transit in Singapore and Bangkok ban the fruit called ___.
Despite its name, this common snack is botanically not a nut at all but a legume in the pea family: the ___.
With nearly 4 percent of it vanishing from stores worldwide each year, the most stolen food on Earth is ___.
Before Dutch growers bred the familiar orange variety, cultivated carrots were mostly yellow and ___.
Thanks to a protein called miraculin, one bite of this West African berry makes sour foods taste sweet: the ___.
Botanically, apples, pears, and cherries all belong to the same plant family as the ___.
Long before coins, the peoples of Mesoamerica used these dried beans as everyday money: ___ beans.
The red food coloring called carmine is made from crushed, dried ___ insects.
Since 2023, the Guinness World Record for the hottest chili pepper has been held by a variety called ___.
The traditional Sardinian cheese casu marzu is intentionally made to contain live ___.
The prized coffee kopi luwak is made from beans eaten and then excreted by an animal called the ___.
The two M's in M&M's come from candy makers Forrest Mars and Bruce ___.
Rhubarb stalks are edible, but the leaves are poisonous because they contain ___.
Botanically a strawberry is not a true berry, but surprisingly a ___ is.
